description: Added Acceptance Criteria section, Agent-Verifiable verification tier, and completion gate in AGENTS.md. 13 assertions per eval (up from 11).

key_findings: With-skill specs include Acceptance Criteria in 3/3 evals (100%). Without-skill specs include it in 0/3 (0%). This is the highest-signal new assertion.
With-skill specs include Agent-Verifiable verification in 3/3 evals (100%). Without-skill specs include it in 0/3 (0%).
Without-skill specs regressed significantly from iteration-5 baseline (88% → 44%) because the 2 new structural assertions are impossible to pass without the template.
With-skill specs maintained high quality (97% → 95%) despite 2 additional assertions raising the bar from 11 to 13.
The 2 with-skill failures are minor: dashboard-chat slightly over 120 lines (128), annotations named specific hooks in Design Decisions.
Without-skill specs had code fences in 2/3 evals, missing Failure Modes in 3/3, and file paths in scope in 1/3 — same anti-patterns as iteration 5.

comparison_with_previous_iterations.analysis: The new assertions (Acceptance Criteria, Agent-Verifiable verification) are structural features that only the skill's template produces. This makes the skill dramatically more valuable — the delta widened from +9pp to +51pp. The with-skill score dropped only 2pp despite adding 2 harder assertions.

new_assertions_impact.note: These 2 assertions account for 6 of the 8 additional failures in the without-skill configuration vs iteration-5. The remaining 2 failures are has_all_sections (now requires Acceptance Criteria heading) and failure_modes_are_policy (now requires Failure Modes subsection).
